faqs.question: How much does car detailing cost in Columbia SC?
faqs.answer: Car detailing in Columbia SC starts at $225 for our Basic Detail package. A full interior and exterior Factory Reset package is $400. Ceramic coating and paint correction pricing is provided by quote depending on the vehicle's size and condition. Most customers spend between $225 and $400 for a complete detail.

faqs.question: How long does a full car detail take in Columbia SC?
faqs.answer: A Basic Detail typically takes 2 to 3 hours. Our Factory Reset full detail takes 4 to 6 hours depending on vehicle size and condition. Ceramic coating and paint correction services can take a full day. We always give you an honest time estimate before we start.

faqs.question: Do you offer mobile car detailing in Columbia SC?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer mobile car detailing throughout Columbia SC and the surrounding Midlands area. We come to your home, office, or any convenient location with all the equipment needed to complete a professional detail on-site. No need to drop off or wait at a shop.

faqs.question: What is the difference between a car wash and car detailing?
faqs.answer: A car wash removes surface dirt and takes 15 to 30 minutes. Professional car detailing is a thorough cleaning and restoration process that takes several hours. It includes paint decontamination, paint correction, deep interior cleaning, leather conditioning, protective coatings, and attention to every surface a car wash never touches.

faqs.question: How often should I get my car detailed in Columbia SC?
faqs.answer: Given Columbia's climate — intense UV, high humidity, heavy pollen, and red clay — we recommend a full detail every 3 to 4 months to maintain your paint and interior. Vehicles with ceramic coating can go longer between details, typically 6 months or more for the exterior.

content:
Car Detailing in Columbia SC: What It Actually Includes

If you've searched for car detailing Columbia SC and wondered what you're actually paying for, you're not alone. The term gets used loosely, but professional detailing is a fundamentally different service from a basic car wash.

A car wash cleans the surface. Car detailing restores the vehicle. A professional detail on your Columbia SC car includes:

  • Paint decontamination with a clay bar to remove bonded contaminants the wash can't touch
  • Careful machine polishing to correct swirl marks, light scratches, and oxidation
  • Protective coating — wax, sealant, or ceramic — applied to the paint surface
  • Deep interior cleaning including steam, extraction, and every crevice in the cabin
  • Leather conditioning, fabric protection, and odor elimination
  • Glass decontamination and treatment inside and out
  • Wheels, wheel wells, tires, and trim restored and protected

The result isn't just clean — it's restored. Paint looks deeper and more reflective. The interior smells and feels new. And protective coatings mean the vehicle stays cleaner longer between services.

Learn more about what each service covers on our interior detailing and exterior detailing pages.

Columbia SC Car Detailing Prices in 2026

Pricing for car detailing in Columbia SC varies by package, vehicle size, and condition. Here's a straightforward look at what you can expect from Mikah's Mobile Detailing:

Package What's Included Starting Price
Basic Detail Hand wash, vacuum, windows, wipe-down, tire dressing $225
Factory Reset Full interior and exterior detail, clay bar, hand wax, steam clean, leather condition $400
Ceramic Coating Full exterior prep, paint correction, professional ceramic coating application Quote
Paint Correction Multi-stage machine polish to remove swirls, scratches, and oxidation Quote

Ceramic coating and paint correction pricing depends heavily on the vehicle's size, paint condition, and the level of correction needed. Contact us for an accurate quote — we don't believe in hidden fees or surprises.

View our full service lineup on the Columbia SC location page.

Interior vs. Exterior Detailing — Do You Need Both?

One of the most common questions we hear from Columbia SC customers is whether they need both interior and exterior work, or whether one alone is worth it. The honest answer: it depends on your situation.

When exterior-only makes sense: Your cabin is in good shape but the paint has dulled, swirls are visible, or pollen and road grime have built up. A clay bar treatment and fresh wax or ceramic coating will make a dramatic difference without touching the interior.

When interior-only makes sense: The outside is presentable, but the inside has pet hair, stains, odors, or just the accumulated grime of daily driving. A deep interior detail with steam cleaning and extraction can make an old cabin feel new again.

When you need both: If it has been more than six months since your last detail, or if you're preparing to sell the vehicle, a full Factory Reset package that covers everything front to back is almost always the right call. In Columbia's climate, both surfaces take a beating — and both benefit from the same session.

How Columbia's Climate Affects Your Car

Columbia SC is not a gentle environment for vehicles. Understanding what your car faces here explains why regular auto detailing in Columbia SC matters more than it does in milder climates.

UV damage: Columbia averages over 215 sunny days per year. Ultraviolet radiation degrades clear coat, fades paint, and cracks leather and vinyl. Without protective coatings, the sun does permanent damage over time. A quality wax or ceramic coating is the most effective defense.

Pollen: The Midlands sees heavy pollen seasons in spring and fall. Oak, pine, and grass pollen are mildly acidic and bond to paint surfaces if left sitting. Regular washing and a sealed paint surface prevent etching.

Red clay: South Carolina's red clay soil is silica-heavy and abrasive. It doesn't just wash off — it bonds to paint, wheel faces, and rubber trim. Clay bar treatment removes it properly without scratching.

Humidity and heat: Columbia summers combine 90-plus-degree heat with high humidity. This accelerates mold growth in interiors, degrades rubber seals, and causes plastics to off-gas oils that leave a greasy film on windows. Regular interior detailing with steam eliminates mold spores before they become a real problem.

Drivers who move to Columbia from the Northeast or Midwest are often surprised how quickly their vehicles deteriorate here. A detail schedule of every 3 to 4 months is not upselling — it's honest advice for this specific climate.

Mobile vs. Shop Detailing in Columbia SC

Both options have real value, and the right choice depends on your priorities.

Shop-based detailing gives the detailer access to a controlled indoor environment — no wind, no rain, covered lighting. For services like ceramic coating, an enclosed space matters because dust contamination during curing is a real risk.

Mobile detailing has grown significantly in popularity in the Columbia SC market, and for most services, it produces identical results. A professional mobile detailer brings the same equipment, the same products, and the same techniques to your driveway or parking spot. What you gain is significant convenience — no drop-off, no waiting room, no arranging a ride home.

For busy households and professionals in areas like Forest Acres, Shandon, Irmo, and Lexington, mobile service means the detail happens while you work, while you're at the gym, or while your kids are at school. The vehicle comes back to you — you never leave.

For ceramic coating, we recommend discussing the environment with us beforehand. We can advise on whether your location is suitable or whether we should plan accordingly.

How to Choose a Car Detailer in Columbia SC

Not every detailer offering car detailing Columbia SC delivers the same quality. Here's what to look for before you book:

Check reviews across multiple platforms. Google, Yelp, and Facebook reviews each attract different customers. A detailer with 4.8 stars across 80 or more reviews on Google has earned that rating over time and across a wide range of jobs. Be skeptical of businesses with very few reviews or a pattern of similar-sounding five-star reviews.

Ask about insurance. Any professional detailer working on your vehicle should carry general liability insurance. If something goes wrong — a scratch, a spill, damage to a component — you want to know they're covered. Don't be shy about asking directly.

Look for IDA certification. The International Detailing Association offers training and certification programs. IDA-certified detailers have demonstrated knowledge of safe paint correction techniques, proper chemical use, and professional standards. It's not a guarantee of quality, but it signals seriousness.

Demand pricing transparency. A reputable detailer will tell you the price before they start, not after. If you're getting a quote, make sure you understand exactly what's included and what could add cost (like heavy pet hair, severe staining, or oversized vehicles). Surprises on the invoice are a bad sign.

Mobile vs. fixed location. As noted above, mobile is the right choice for most customers and most services. If you're considering ceramic coating or multi-stage paint correction, ask the detailer about their process and environment. A professional will give you a straight answer.

Ready to book? Visit our Columbia SC page or call (803) 667-8731 to schedule your detail.
